calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Aug-06-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Bernardo Silva for Robert Sanchez (Petitioner/Personal Representative).
Paul Mori for Elizabeth Sanchez (Beneficiary).

Mr. Mori stated that his Client is not objecting to the petition. The Court raised the issue of the division between ordinary and extraordinary time stating that there are very similar entries for similar tasks that seem to be ordinary services, not extraordinary. Mr. Silva stated that he had to deal with the issue of the use of the trademark for the five siblings and their separate businesses, that he had to make sure the siblings were adequately insured and that they were conducting their businesses properly, and that the work was very similar and very difficult to divide out. Mr. Silva also stated that he tried to minimize extraordinary time and put some into ordinary services. Mr. Mori stated that his Client is not objecting to fees, that it was a complicated situation with the trademark, and that his Client is getting the Silver Street property and has been waiting for some time to get financing. The Fifth Amended Accounting Petition, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: B. Wan. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jun-11-2019 Continued To Jul-09-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar May-14-2019 Continued To Jun-11-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Bernardo Silva for Robert Sanchez (Petitioner/Executor).
Maureen Harrington for Robert Sanchez (individually).

The Court questions whether adequate notice was provided to Claimant West Asset Management, stated that by its count only 14 days notice was given, and that its concern is that the Claimant is entitled to notice and to conclude that this was inadequate notice. Mr. Silva stated that he thought he was required to give ten days notice, that he tried to contact them, that he was told they no longer handle the account, that it is deemed as rejection and that the claim is barred by the statute of limitations. The Petition for Final Distribution, etc. is continued to June 11, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Counsel to provide proper notice.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Oct-16-2017 Continued To Mar-05-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Bernardo Silva for and with Robert Sanchez.
Paul Mori for Elizabeth Sanchez.

Mr. Silva stated that there is a home on Silver Avenue and a business trade mark that is being used by the five heirs and their businesses, that there is the issue of whether the business on 24th Street should be included in the Estate or pass to the individuals, that there have been discussions as to changing to a single business entity, that they have a business meeting coming up and that it is in the best interest of the Estate to approve the stipulation to extend time for filing of the petition for final distribution. Status on filing of a petition for final distribution is continued to March 5, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If a petition for final distribution is not doable by January 8, 2018, the Parties are to notify the Court and file a petition for approval of report on status of administration. Status on filing of the said petition is set for January 29, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204

Judge: Andrew Y. S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported
